name: Olist Brazilian Ecommerce Data Product
description: ~100k orders, Brazilian marketplace, 2016-2018. Full order lifecycle: purchase → payment → fulfillment → delivery → review. Use for: order volume/revenue trends, seller performance, payment analysis, geographic demand, delivery KPIs, product category popularity, customer satisfaction, freight costs. DO NOT use for: real-time inventory, customer identity resolution (IDs anonymized), profitability/profit margin (no cost data), returns/refunds, budget/targets, marketing attribution, data outside 2016-2018. Monetary values in BRL. Net revenue = exclude order_status='canceled', exclude freight. Gross revenue = all orders regardless of status, include both price and freight.

tables.description: Central fact table. One row per order. Hub connecting all other tables. Join to order_items ON order_id (items/price/seller), payments ON order_id (method/amount), reviews ON order_id (score), customers ON customer_id (location). Use for order counts, status distribution, delivery timeliness, time-series. Not for item/product detail.
columns: PK. Join key for order_items, payments, reviews.
FK to customers. Order-scoped — use customer_unique_id in customers for cross-order customer counts.
Values: delivered, shipped, canceled, unavailable, invoiced, processing, created, approved. Filter to 'delivered' for completed orders; exclude 'canceled' for net revenue.
When customer placed order. Primary time dimension. Cast to TIMESTAMP for date ops.
Payment approval time. Nullable. Diff with purchase_timestamp = processing lag.
When seller handed to carrier. Nullable. Diff with approved_at = seller processing time.
Actual delivery to customer. Nullable. Compare to estimated_delivery_date for on-time/late.
Estimated delivery shown to customer at purchase. Orders delivered after this = late.

tables.description: Item-level fact. One row per order+item sequence. Join to orders ON order_id, products ON product_id, sellers ON seller_id. Use for revenue (price+freight), product/seller performance, item counts, freight analysis. Not for payment method. One order can have items from multiple sellers.
columns: Item sequence within order (1,2,3…). With order_id forms grain.
FK to sellers. Different items in same order may have different sellers.
Seller's deadline to hand item to carrier.
Item sale price in BRL, excl. freight.
Shipping cost per item in BRL. price+freight_value = total item cost.

tables.description: Product dimension. One row per product. Join to order_items ON product_id. Use for category revenue/volume, product attributes, top-selling categories. Category names in Portuguese.
columns: Category in Portuguese (e.g. beleza_saude=health&beauty, informatica_acessorios=computer accessories, cama_mesa_banho=bed/bath). Nullable.
Name char length. Typo in col name ('lenght'). Nullable.
Description char length. Typo in col name ('lenght'). Nullable.

tables.description: Customer dimension. One row per customer_id (order-scoped). Join to orders ON customer_id. Use customer_unique_id (not customer_id) to count distinct real customers across orders. Use for geographic analysis, regional demand.
columns: PK. Order-scoped — NOT a true unique customer ID. Use customer_unique_id for deduplication.
True unique customer ID across orders. Use for distinct customer counts and repeat purchase analysis.
Customer city, lowercase. Most common: sao paulo.
2-letter state code (SP=São Paulo, RJ=Rio de Janeiro, MG=Minas Gerais).

tables.description: Seller dimension. One row per seller. Join to order_items ON seller_id. Use for seller location, geographic distribution, seller counts by city/state.
columns: First 5 digits of seller zip code.

tables.description: Payment fact. One row per payment installment. An order can have multiple rows (multiple methods or installments). Join to orders ON order_id. Use for payment method analysis, installment behavior, total payment value (SUM payment_value GROUP BY order_id). Not for item-level revenue (use order_items.price).
columns: FK to orders. One order can have multiple payment rows.
Method: credit_card, boleto (bank slip), debit_card, voucher, not_defined. Credit card most common.
Number of installments. Brazilians commonly split into 2-12 monthly payments.
Payment amount in BRL. SUM per order_id for order total. May differ slightly from order_items sum due to vouchers/rounding.

tables.description: Review fact. One row per review (typically one per order). Join to orders ON order_id. Use for satisfaction scores, avg rating by category/seller/region, response time. Comments in Portuguese, often null.
columns: Review title in Portuguese. Often null.
Review body in Portuguese. Often null.
